spec_match.rationale: Page hooks `beforeinput` and calls preventDefault to inject sanitised HTML — that's a perfectly valid editor pattern, but it's the pre-v143 path (beforeinput already had dataTransfer). The v143 change is about populating dataTransfer on the `input` event, which the page never listens to. Demo would behave identically on Chrome 142.

interactive.rationale: Live contenteditable with four sanitiser toggles (strip-style / strip-img / keep-links / plain-text) that change real paste behaviour. 6 controls counted.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb says 'read dataTransfer on beforeinput, cancel the default, and insert a cleaned-up version.' That's the page — but it's not the v143 feature; the feature is about input, not beforeinput. Blurb misframes the change.

edge_cases.rationale: Handles missing dataTransfer (logs 'pre-v143') and falls through to default. Doesn't show what happens on the `input` event after sanitisation, doesn't probe the contenteditable-only scope, and uses document.execCommand which is deprecated.

references.rationale: Page contains https://chromestatus.com/feature/6715253274181632 and the input-events spec.

openQuestions.title: Pivot the demo to the `input` event
openQuestions.detail: The v143 feature is dataTransfer-on-input. Currently the page sanitises on beforeinput, which works on every Chrome since the inputType vocabulary shipped. Restructure so the sanitiser runs on `input` (where the new dataTransfer landed), with beforeinput shown for contrast: 'beforeinput → had it always; input → has it from v143'. Then sanitisation-on-input becomes the actual v143 demo.

openQuestions.title: Replace document.execCommand('insertHTML', …)
openQuestions.detail: execCommand is deprecated in W3C and unreliable across browsers. Replace with the Selection / Range API: `getSelection().getRangeAt(0).insertNode(parsedFragment)` after clearing the range. Editor demos in 2026 should not be teaching execCommand.

openQuestions.title: Show the textarea-no-effect counterexample
openQuestions.detail: The chromestatus blurb says the feature is contenteditable-only — textarea/input still see null dataTransfer on input events. A small adjacent textarea panel would teach the scope and stop devs assuming the feature applies everywhere.

openQuestions.title: Add a 'paste from Word' test snippet
openQuestions.detail: Blurb references 'paste from Word, Google Docs, or any HTML source'. Including a small 'Copy me' button that places a known-messy HTML payload on the clipboard would let visitors test the sanitiser without leaving the page. Use the Clipboard API if available, fall back to a textarea with the markup.

summary: Genuinely useful editor pattern but doesn't actually demonstrate the v143 change — it sanitises on beforeinput, which has had dataTransfer since the inputType vocabulary shipped. Move the sanitiser to the `input` event, keep beforeinput as the contrast, and drop execCommand for the modern Range API.
